title: galium aparine subsp. tenerum
intro: Galium aparine subsp. tenerum, also known as sticky cleaver or climbing bedstraw, is a subspecies of perennial herb that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Galium aparine subsp. tenerum include reducing fever, alleviating skin irritations, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and used in capsules or as a powder, to harness its medicinal properties.
